title: pteridium pinetorum subsp. osmundaceum
intro: Pteridium pinetorum subsp. osmundaceum, also known as bracken fern, is a subspecies of fern native to New Zealand. It is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Dennstaedtiaceae and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Pteridium pinetorum subsp. osmundaceum include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of bracken fern can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote digestive health, relieve respiratory issues, and support the immune system.
